title: angorkis caudata
intro: Angorkis caudata, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the family Asclepiadaceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. Consuming Angorkis caudata has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
